# Figure description: Figure 4.png

**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type
Geological cross-section (vertical section) showing drill hole traces, mineralised intervals, and an interpreted mineralised envelope beneath an open pit mine.

---

## Coverage and Subject
The figure covers the **Oxonia Pit** area, owned by **Fairbairn Goldfields**, located in the Murchison region of Western Australia. The section is oriented along **northing 12135N**, covering an easting range of approximately **9950mE to 10000mE**. The vertical depth range extends from **0m RL (surface/pit floor level)** to approximately **120m RL below surface**. Multiple drill holes are shown, identified as: **AEMAC13, AEMAC12, AEMAC06, AEMAC10, AEMAC11, EMR08, AEMAC05, AEMAC01**, plus a **Proposed Drill Hole** (dashed line).

---

## Key Content

**Open Pit Geometry:**
The Oxonia Pit is depicted at the top of the section as a V-shaped excavation cutting into the ground surface.

**Drill Holes:**
Multiple sub-vertical drill holes are shown penetrating to varying depths. Key depths noted are 19m, 20m, 28m, 39m, 80m, 95m, and 120m. Several holes are annotated with **"No Sig Assays"** (no significant assays), indicating barren or sub-economic intersections in those drill traces.

**Mineralised Intervals (highlighted in red/pink):**
Significant gold mineralisation is shown as red/pink elongated lens-shaped bodies. The assay results annotated are:
- **3m @ 3.6 g/t Au** — shallow intersection, left-centre of section (~AEMAC06/AEMAC10 area)
- **6m @ 5.5 g/t Au** — shallow intersection, central-right area (~AEMAC11/EMR08)
- **2m @ 2.9 g/t Au** — mid-depth, left-centre (~AEMAC10, ~28m depth)
- **1m @ 2.0 g/t Au** — shallow, right-centre (~AEMAC05)
- **6m @ 17.9 g/t Au** — highlighted in **yellow box** (highest grade result), right-centre area (~AEMAC05, ~19–25m depth)
- **10m @ 2.2 g/t Au** — deeper intersection, central area (~AEMAC11, ~39m depth)

**Interpreted Mineralised Envelope:**
A **dashed curved line** labelled *"Interpreted Mineralised Envelope"* outlines the inferred extent of gold mineralisation. This envelope dips moderately to the east/southeast and remains **OPEN at depth** (indicated by a bold red arrow and the word **"OPEN"** at ~39–50m depth).

**Proposed Drill Hole:**
A dashed straight line on the right side of the section represents a **proposed drill hole** targeting the interpreted mineralised envelope at depth, aimed at testing the down-dip extension.

**Legend (bottom left):**
- Red rectangle = Mineralised Interval
- Line = Drill Trace
- Tick mark with depth number = Drill Depth

---

## Text and Labels

- **Title:** Fairbairn Goldfields / Oxonia Pit / Section 12135N
- **Easting coordinates:** 9950mE (left), 10000mE (right)
- **Depth/RL labels:** 0m RL, 20m RL, 40m RL, 60m RL, 80m RL, 100m RL (left axis)
- **Drill hole IDs:** AEMAC13, AEMAC12, AEMAC06, AEMAC10, AEMAC11, EMR08, AEMAC05, AEMAC01
- **Assay annotations:** 3m @ 3.6 g/t Au; 6m @ 5.5 g/t Au; 2m @ 2.9 g/t Au; 1m @ 2.0 g/t Au; **6m @ 17.9 g/t Au** (yellow highlight); 10m @ 2.2 g/t Au
- **Depth tick annotations:** 19m, 20m, 28m, 39m (×3), 80m, 95m, 120m
- **Annotations:** "No Sig Assays" (multiple holes); "OPEN" (red arrow); "Interpreted Mineralised Envelope"; "Proposed Drill Hole"
- **Scale bar:** 0–25m

---

## Research Value

This cross-section demonstrates that **high-grade gold mineralisation** (up to 6m @ 17.9 g/t Au) exists immediately below and adjacent to the Oxonia Pit floor, hosted within a moderately dipping mineralised envelope that remains **open at depth**, indicating strong potential for additional resources beneath the current pit shell. The figure directly answers whether near-pit, down-dip gold targets exist, and the proposed drill hole trajectory shows the company's intention to test this extension systematically. For a research assistant, this section is critical evidence of **near-surface, high-grade gold continuity** at Oxonia Pit and justifies further resource drilling to evaluate underground or deeper open pit mining potential.
